title: psorospermum lanceolatum
intro: Psorospermum lanceolatum, also known as the quinine tree, is a plant species native to Madagascar and other tropical regions. The plant's bark contains a chemical called quinine, which has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, including antimalarial and antipyretic effects. The quinine in Psorospermum lanceolatum has been used to treat various health conditions, including malaria, fever, and arthritis, as well as to reduce inflammation and ease pain. Herbal preparations of Psorospermum lanceolatum include quinine-rich extracts, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
